intro: Canada is proposing restrictions on social media for under-16s and AI regulation, with penalties reaching 3 percent of global revenue for non-compliance. Jeff Bezos, serving as co-CEO of physical AI startup Prometheus after its $12 billion funding round, compared AI to a knife that can be used for good or bad purposes. OpenAI is considering significant price cuts to compete with Anthropic and Google as businesses resist high AI service costs.

whitelist.title: Artificial intelligence: Latest News and Updates | South China Morning Post
whitelist.desc: News articles, analyses and commentary about machines that mimic human cognitive functions such as learning and problem solving. This also covers chatbots, algorithms, consumer products and Big Data analysis that make use of AI.

whitelist.title: Canada moves to ban under-16s from social media, regulate AI
whitelist.desc: The proposed plan could see companies fined up to 3 per cent of their global revenue or US$7.2 million if they do not comply.

whitelist.title: Jeff Bezos says AI is like a knife that can be used for good or bad, and the solution isn't 'no more data centers'
whitelist.desc: The Amazon founder is serving as the co-CEO of Prometheus, a physical AI startup that just closed a $12 billion round.

whitelist.title: Deezer is fighting against slop with a tool that detects AI music on streaming platforms

And now, they’re reportedly putting more energy into producing “short episodic series using existing Lionsgate IP.” [Link: Lionsgate Takes Equity Stake in Runway, Will Mine IP for AI-Generated Shorts | https://www.thewrap.com/creative-content/movies/lionsgate-runway-ai-short-films-franchises-ip/ | TheWrap]
